name: Tityridae
description: Tityridae is a family of Neotropical passerines that includes tityras, becards, and mourners. They are medium-sized birds with strong bills and a variety of plumage patterns.

children.name: Tityra
children.description: Tityra is a genus of medium-sized, primarily black-and-white birds with bare red facial skin around the eyes.

children.name: Tityra cayana
children.description: The black-tailed tityra is a striking black-and-white bird with a black tail and red orbital skin, found across Amazonia and the Guianas. It often perches conspicuously on exposed branches.

children.name: Tityra inquisitor
children.description: The black-crowned tityra is a smaller tityra with a black crown and white underparts, ranging from Mexico to Argentina. It is often found in open woodlands and forest edges.

children.name: Tityra semifasciata
children.description: The masked tityra has a black face mask and broad white wing patches visible in flight. It ranges from Mexico to Bolivia and is the most widespread tityra species.

children.name: Pachyramphus
children.description: Pachyramphus is a genus of stocky, large-headed birds with slightly hooked bills, found from Mexico to Argentina.

children.name: Pachyramphus aglaiae
children.description: The rose-throated becard is a small songbird with a rosy pink throat patch in males, found from southern Texas to Costa Rica. It builds a distinctive hanging pouch-like nest.

children.name: Pachyramphus validus
children.description: The crested becard is a large, black-and-white becard with a prominent crest, found from Peru to Argentina. It inhabits forest edges and open woodlands in lowland and foothill regions.

children.name: Pachyramphus versicolor
children.description: A small becard with barred underparts, found from Costa Rica to Bolivia.

children.name: Pachyramphus spodiurus
children.description: A slaty-grey becard found in the foothills of the Andes from Colombia to Peru.

children.name: Pachyramphus rufus
children.description: A small becard with grey upperparts and pale underparts, found in northern South America.

children.name: Pachyramphus major
children.description: A becard with grey collar, found from Mexico to Honduras.

children.name: Pachyramphus xanthogenys
children.description: A becard with yellow cheeks, found in the western Amazon basin.

children.name: Pachyramphus violaceus
children.description: A violaceous becard found in the Amazon and Orinoco basins.

children.name: Pachyramphus viridis
children.description: A becard with green back and yellow underparts, found in southern South America.

children.name: Pachyramphus surinamus
children.description: A glossy-backed becard found in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Pachyramphus cinnamomeus
children.description: A becard with warm cinnamon plumage, found from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Pachyramphus homopterus
children.description: A uniformly coloured becard found in the Guiana Shield and northern Amazon.

children.name: Pachyramphus marginatus
children.description: A becard with black cap and white collar, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Pachyramphus castaneus
children.description: A becard with chestnut crown, found in eastern South America.

children.name: Pachyramphus albescens
children.description: A becard from the southern Amazon.

children.name: Pachyramphus cavanus
children.description: A becard from the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Pachyramphus nanus
children.description: A tiny becard from the western Amazon.

children.name: Pachyramphus parvus
children.description: A becard from the western Amazon.

children.name: Laniocera
children.description: A genus of medium-sized tityrids with spotted plumage, found in lowland forests of Central and South America.

children.name: Laniocera hypopyrra
children.description: A medium-sized passerine with grey-brown plumage and rufous wing patches, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Laniocera rufescens
children.description: A passerine with heavily spotted underparts, found from southern Mexico to Colombia.

children.name: Laniisoma
children.description: A genus of tityrids with shrike-like hooked bills from South America.

children.name: Laniisoma elegans
children.description: A cotinga-like bird from the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, considered vulnerable.

children.name: Laniisoma buckleyi
children.description: A shrike-like cotinga from the western Amazon.

children.name: Iodopleura
children.description: Tiny tityrids with purple flank tufts from the Amazon.

children.name: Iodopleura pipra
children.description: A tiny passerine with purple flank tufts from the Amazon.

children.name: Iodopleura leucopygia
children.description: A tiny passerine from the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Iodopleura isabellae
children.description: A tiny passerine from the western Amazon.
